zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Houghton於大約1390年出生在英國藍卡爾麗婭 霍爾時，他的父親Sir William de Houghton39歲，母親Margaret Alice Banastre20歲。他和Lady Margaret Richmond至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1468年在英國藍卡爾麗婭 霍爾享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Houghton于大约1390年出生在英国藍卡爾麗婭 霍爾时，他的父亲Sir William de Houghton39岁，母亲Margaret Alice Banastre20岁。他和Lady Margaret Richmond至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1468年在英国藍卡爾麗婭 霍爾享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Houghton was born about 1390, in Lea Hall, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir William de Houghton, was 39 and his mother, Margaret Alice Banastre, was 20. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Lady Margaret Richmond. He died in 1468, in Lea Hall, Lancashire, England, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Houghton est né environ 1390, à Lea Hall, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Houghton, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Margaret Alice Banastre, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Lady Margaret Richmond. Il est décédé en 1468, à Lea Hall, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Houghton um 1390 in Lea Hall, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de Houghton 39 und seine Mutter Margaret Alice Banastre 20. Er hatte mit Lady Margaret Richmond mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1468 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Lea Hall, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Houghton è nato il circa 1390 nel Lea Hall, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Sir William de Houghton, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Alice Banastre, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Lady Margaret Richmond. Morì nel 1468, nel Lea Hall, Lancashire, England, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghtonが約1390年にイギリスランカシュアイアレア ハルで生まれた時，父Sir William de Houghtonは39歳，母Margaret Alice Banastreは20歳であった。彼はLady Margaret Richmondとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1468年にイギリスランカシュアイアレア ハルで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Houghton(이)가 대략 1390년 영국 랭카실르 리아 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Houghton(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Alice Banastre(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Lady Margaret Richmond(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1468년 영국 랭카실르 리아 홀에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Houghton nasceu aproximadamente 1390, em Lea Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Houghton, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Alice Banastre, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Lady Margaret Richmond. Ele faleceu em 1468, em Lea Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Houghton родился около 1390 г. в местности леа холл, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William de Houghton было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Alice Banastre, 20. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Lady Margaret Richmond. Он умер в 1468 г. в местности леа холл, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Houghton nació aproximadamente 1390, en Lea Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Houghton, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Margaret Alice Banastre, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Lady Margaret Richmond. Murió en 1468, en Lea Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 79 años.
